Defines functions and constants for erasing and writing to flash memory with progress callbacks.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that provides functionality for managing flash memory on an STM32F2 microcontroller. It defines constants for the base addresses of various flash memory sectors, which are used to identify specific regions of the flash memory. The file includes two primary functions: [`system_flash_erase`](<#system_flash_erase>) and [`system_flash_write`](<#system_flash_write>). These functions allow for erasing and writing data to the flash memory, respectively. Both functions accept a callback function, `SystemFlashProgressCb`, which can be used to monitor the progress of the operations.

The [`system_flash_erase`](<#system_flash_erase>) function erases flash memory sectors that overlap with a specified address range. If any part of a sector is within the range, the entire sector is erased. The [`system_flash_write`](<#system_flash_write>) function writes data to the flash memory, assuming the memory has already been erased. Both functions return a boolean value indicating success or failure. The inclusion of the `stm32f2xx_flash.h` header suggests that this code is intended to work with the STM32F2 series of microcontrollers, and it relies on the STM32 HAL (Hardware Abstraction Layer) for low-level flash memory operations.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
- `string.h`
- `stm32f2xx_flash.h`
